campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. The council also discussed extending a temporary local rule (ordinance 2326) and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant funding to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Extension of interim ordinance 2326
Grant application for Hamilton Avenue bicycle bridge over Highway 17
Feasibility study approval for the bicycle overcrossing project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council decided to move forward with the proposal and skip the usual first reading step, planning to vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next council meeting
Streamlined approval process used to fast-track the proposal

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to plan their upcoming meetings scheduled for April 7 and April 14, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to the agendas, decided which topics need specific time slots for discussion, and checked whether any documents needed special notice to the public.
keyTopics: April 7, 2026 meeting agenda finalization
April 14, 2026 meeting agenda planning
Public notice requirements for agenda items and documents
Scheduling specific time slots for agenda discussions

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council also reviewed committee reports and discussed upcoming meetings for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff updates
Scheduling of board and commission meetings
Documents available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects. The commission approved their previous meeting notes and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal grant applications for affordable housing projects
Community development funding decisions
Human services program funding requests
Approval of February meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council focused on ways to support local businesses and encourage shopping in Cupertino. They discussed a program to help businesses that may not have proper licenses get current without penalties, promoted a summer campaign to encourage residents to shop locally, and explored partnerships with shopping centers and sponsors to fund city events.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helps businesses get compliant without penalties
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local Campaign - encourages residents to support Cupertino businesses
Shopping center outreach and partnerships to boost local retail
Public-Private partnerships to fund city events and reduce city costs
Economic incentives for startups and small businesses in Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Historical and Landmarks Commission is holding a public hearing on April 2, 2026, where residents can participate in person or online via Zoom. The city is accepting written comments from the public about historical and landmarks matters up until noon on the day of the meeting.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission public hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person + Zoom option)
Deadline for written public comments: noon on meeting day
How to join remotely via Zoom or phone

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on March 17, 2026 to handle routine business including approving meeting minutes, reviewing the calendar for upcoming meetings, and authorizing Mayor Montano to attend the WELL 2026 Annual conference. The council also confirmed a school district representative and gave the City Manager authority to negotiate and execute an unspecified agreement.
keyTopics: Mayor Montano's travel authorization to WELL 2026 Annual conference
Appointment of Milpitas Unified School District representative to Economic committee
Approval of previous meeting minutes and confirmation of meeting calendar
City Manager given negotiating authority for undisclosed agreement

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to handle several routine matters. They recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city buildings during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract to buy email security software from Insight Public Sector for about $93,000 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Emergency shelter agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
